reason: GEPA pilot for Charm Person sharpened the spell toward warm, truthful trust-building, disclosure survivability, visible opt-outs, and explicit refusal of manipulative influence tactics.

updated_fields.tagline: Draft warm, honest communication that earns trust without pressure.
updated_fields.description: Use this spell for one-to-one or small-group communication where relationship quality matters: introductions, serious messages, apologies, invitations, or requests that need warmth without false intimacy. Charm Person is about truthful rapport-building that would still look legitimate if forwarded, screenshotted, or read aloud by the recipient. It is not manipulative copywriting, fake urgency, or disguised coercion.
when_to_use: You need to ask for time, attention, forgiveness, or collaboration in a way that respects the recipient’s autonomy.
A socially sensitive message needs warmth, clarity, and a visible no-pressure path if the recipient wants to decline.
You want trust-building communication that survives disclosure rather than relying on hidden tactics or counterfeit intimacy.
The message should be reviewed by a human because the relationship, power dynamic, or pressure risk matters as much as the prose.
workflow: Identify the recipient, the relationship context, the real ask, and any power imbalance or vulnerability that changes what is appropriate.
State the honest reason for the message and the real benefit or legitimate rationale for the recipient, not just the sender.
Draft the message with warmth, specificity, and no-pressure language; include a visible decline or opt-out path when relevant.
Run a manipulation audit and remove fake urgency, deceptive framing, coerced intimacy, or hidden pressure points before sending.
Flag what still needs human review, especially if money, employment, romance, education, or other power dynamics are involved.
deliverables: A warm draft message or short communication plan sized to the relationship and audience.
A note on tone choices, disclosure survivability, and why the draft should still feel legitimate if shared publicly.
A manipulation audit that highlights pressure points removed, plus any human-review flags that remain.
guardrails: Do not use deception, fake urgency, counterfeit intimacy, guilt, or dark patterns.
Do not hide opt-outs, decline paths, sender intent, or material tradeoffs.
Refuse requests that depend on coercion, misrepresentation, parasocial leverage, or exploiting vulnerability.
updated_fields.default_prompt: Use $charm-person to draft a warm, honest message, then run a manipulation audit and flag anything that would fail disclosure or pressure the recipient.
